metadata.Title: Default full pipeline run
metadata.description: Baseline run with no constraints. Validates end-to-end execution across all observation modules. Every returned lead must have a non-generic title, a description with concrete evidence (risk scores, alert names, or privilege state), and at least one observation from any module.

leads.title: Privilege Escalation Corroborated by Critical Alerts
leads.byline: User john.doe@corp.com gained elevated privileges while triggering Credential Access and Defense Evasion alerts.
leads.description: john.doe@corp.com transitioned from a non-privileged to a privileged account state within the last 7 days (temporal escalation). During the same window, 4 critical-severity alerts fired across 2 tactics: 'Credential Access' and 'Defense Evasion'. The entity risk score rose from 38 to 94 (+56 in 24h). The combination of privilege escalation plus high-severity multi-tactic alerting places this entity at priority 9.
chatRecommendations: Show all authentication events for john.doe@corp.com in the last 24 hours
What group membership or role changes occurred for this user in the past 7 days?
List all alerts for john.doe@corp.com grouped by MITRE tactic
What other entities communicated with john.doe@corp.com during the escalation window?
Show the risk score timeline for this user over the last 30 days

metadata.Title: Single top lead — quality reference
metadata.description: Returns only the highest-priority lead. The reference output shows ideal lead quality: entity-specific title naming the threat type, a description that traces an evidence chain across at least 2 observation modules, priority justified by severity, and chat recommendations that are scoped to the specific entity and investigation angle. The actual output should match this depth and specificity.

metadata.Title: Top-5 leads — diversity across modules
metadata.description: Returns 5 leads. Expect output diversity: leads should draw from at least 2 different observation modules (e.g. risk_analysis, behavioral_analysis, temporal_state_analysis). Not all 5 should come from the same entity type. Leads ranked 1-2 should have priority >= 6; leads ranked 4-5 may be lower priority but must still have at least one medium observation. Titles must be distinct — generic titles like 'High Risk Entity' are not acceptable.

metadata.Title: Top-10 leads — priority ordering consistency
metadata.description: Returns 10 leads. Priority scores must be monotonically non-increasing from first to last. The top 3 leads should have at least one critical or high severity observation. Leads at positions 7-10 may have lower priority but each must have at least 1 observation. No two leads should have identical titles or identical entity names unless they represent genuinely different entity types (e.g. a user and a host with the same name).

leads.title: Rapid Risk Score Spike: Host web-srv-04 Jumps 52 Points in 24h
leads.byline: Host web-srv-04 experienced an unprecedented risk score increase alongside a volume spike in medium-severity alerts.
leads.description: web-srv-04 had a baseline risk score of 31 (low). Within the last 24 hours the score rose to 83 (high), a delta of +52. Over the same 24-hour window, 14 medium-severity alerts fired — 3x the entity's 30-day average of 4.6 alerts/day. Rule names include 'Unusual Process Execution', 'Outbound Connection to Rare IP', and 'Large File Transfer'. Priority 7 reflects a sharp, data-driven spike with corroborating alert volume.
chatRecommendations: What processes ran on web-srv-04 in the 2 hours before the risk score spiked?
List all outbound network connections from web-srv-04 in the last 24 hours
Show all alerts for web-srv-04 with the rule names that contributed to the spike
Which user accounts logged in to web-srv-04 during the alert window?

metadata.Title: Top-3 leads — description depth
metadata.description: Returns 3 leads. With a tight cap, each lead should have a rich description that includes at least: the entity name, specific numeric evidence (risk scores, alert counts, or time deltas), and a clear explanation of why the priority score was assigned. A description like 'This entity has a high risk score' without specifics scores 0. Chat recommendations must be entity-specific, not generic security questions.

leads.title: Multi-Tactic Attack Pattern: User sarah.kim Triggers Across 4 MITRE Tactics
leads.byline: User sarah.kim has alerts spanning Initial Access, Persistence, Lateral Movement, and Exfiltration in the last 7 days.
leads.description: sarah.kim's activity over the last 7 days spans 4 distinct MITRE ATT&CK tactics based on 11 matched detection rules. This breadth of tactic coverage (>= 3 distinct tactics) is a strong signal of coordinated or multi-stage attack activity. Individual alerts include 'Phishing Link Clicked' (Initial Access), 'New Scheduled Task Created' (Persistence), 'Pass-the-Hash Attempt' (Lateral Movement), and 'Data Staged for Exfiltration' (Exfiltration). Risk score is 78. Priority 8.
chatRecommendations: Show all alerts for sarah.kim grouped by MITRE tactic over the last 14 days
What assets did sarah.kim access during the Lateral Movement alerts?
Show sarah.kim's login history and source IPs over the last 7 days
Were any other users involved in the same alert rules that triggered for sarah.kim?
What data was staged and where did it go during the Exfiltration alert?

metadata.Title: Multi-tactic detection — chat recommendation quality
metadata.description: Returns 5 leads. Focus evaluation on chat recommendations: each lead's recommendations should be specific to the entity's observed tactics and evidence, not generic security questions. A recommendation like 'Show recent alerts' scores lower than 'Show alerts for [entity] grouped by MITRE tactic in the last 7 days'. Recommendations should enable a SOC analyst to immediately begin investigating without reformulating the question.

leads.title: Privileged Entity admin-svc-account at Persistent Critical Risk
leads.byline: Service account admin-svc-account has maintained a critical risk score (>= 90) for 90 days while retaining privileged status.
leads.description: admin-svc-account is flagged as Privileged (entity.attributes.Privileged=true) and has had a normalized risk score >= 90 continuously for 90 days. This persistent high-risk posture for a privileged entity represents a significant unresolved threat. The entity appears to have no associated human identity (no user.full_name or user.email), suggesting it may be a shared service account. Priority 10 is assigned for the combination of privileged status and persistent critical risk.
chatRecommendations: What services or systems authenticate using admin-svc-account?
Show all actions taken by admin-svc-account in the last 30 days
Has the password or credentials for admin-svc-account been rotated in the last 90 days?
Which humans last used or configured admin-svc-account?
Are there any anomalies in the login times or source IPs for admin-svc-account?

metadata.Title: Privileged entity risk — priority justification
metadata.description: Returns 5 leads. Focus evaluation on whether priority scores are plausible and well-justified. A priority of 8-10 should only appear for entities with critical-severity observations or multi-module corroboration. A priority of 3-5 should be justified by medium observations. Leads where the priority number does not match the severity of the listed observations score 0 on this criterion. The reference shows a priority-10 lead that is fully justified.

metadata.Title: Large batch — structural completeness at scale
metadata.description: Returns up to 20 leads. At this scale, evaluate whether ALL leads (not just the top ones) maintain structural completeness: each must have id, title, byline, description, entities (non-empty array), priority (1-10), observations (non-empty array), and executionUuid. Titles must not repeat verbatim across leads. Lower-priority leads (positions 15-20) may have shorter descriptions but must still name the entity and at least one specific observation.
